title: Mali Rebels Seize Tessalit Military Base as JNIM Establishes Checkpoints Near Bamako
summary: Al-Qaeda-linked JNIM fighters and Tuareg separatists have seized the Tessalit military base in northern Mali and established checkpoints around the capital Bamako, marking a dramatic escalation in the Mali security crisis following the killing of Defense Minister Sadio Camara.
contents:
## Mali Security Crisis Deepens

### Situation Overview
Al-Qaeda-linked Jamaat Nusrat al-Islam wal-Muslimin (JNIM) and Tuareg separatists from the Azawad Liberation Front (FLA) have seized the Tessalit military base in northern Mali and established checkpoints near the capital Bamako.

### Key Developments
- JNIM called on Malians to rise up to "bring down the junta" and adopt Islamic law
- The FLA raised its flag over the Amachach base in Tessalit following withdrawal of Malian army and Russian African Corps forces
- JNIM earlier captured the base of Hombori in central Mali and two checkpoints near Bamako
- Four major military camps in northern Mali now sit under armed group control
- The Alliance of Sahel States (AES) — Mali, Burkina Faso, and Niger — conducted "intense air campaigns" in response
- Russia's African Corps denied abandoning Hombori, claiming helicopter ammunition deliveries continued

### Background
The attacks represent the largest assault on Mali in nearly 15 years, following the killing of Defense Minister Sadio Camara. Mali's military junta, which seized power through coups in 2020-2021, faces its most severe security challenge. About 1,000 people gathered in Niamey, Niger to express solidarity with Mali.

### Regional Impact
Burkina Faso Defense Minister Celestin Simpore vowed at Camara's funeral to "hunt down" the attackers. Niger authorities accused foreign powers, primarily France, of sponsoring the attacks.
